16.bio: Albert Chon is Co-founder and CTO of Injective Protocol, building the future of decentralized exchange. He was formerly a Consultant at OpenZeppelin and a Software Engineer at Amazon. For fun, he enjoys painting, skiing, photography, and learning about crypto, ML, cybersecurity, math, finance, psychology, longevity, cool tech, and new ways of thinking.

17.bio: Balaji S. Srinivasan is an angel investor, tech founder, and Wall Street Journal bestselling author of The Network State. He was formerly the CTO of Coinbase and General Partner at Andreessen Horowitz, and is an early investor in many successful tech companies and crypto protocols, including Alchemy, Ava Labs, Bitcoin, Cameo, Chainlink, Clubhouse, Dapper Labs, Ethereum, Instadapp, NEAR Protocol, OnDeck, Opensea, Solana, Soylent, Superhuman, Synthesis, and Zora. Dr. Srinivasan is also the cofounder of Earn.com (acquired by Coinbase), Counsyl (acquired by Myriad), Teleport (acquired by Topia), and Coin Center. He holds a BS/MS/PhD in Electrical Engineering and an MS in Chemical Engineering from Stanford University.

18.bio: Lily Liu is the Chair of the Solana Foundation, co-founder of Anagram, and a board member for several projects. Previously, she was the co-founder and CFO of Earn.com. Lily Liu is a graduate of Stanford University and Harvard University.

19.bio: Ben Fisch is currently the Co-founder,CEO, CTO at Espresso Systems. He graduated from Stanford University.

20.bio: Laura Shin is the founder and CEO of Unchained and author of “The Cryptopians: Idealism, Greed, Lies, and the Making of the First Big Cryptocurrency Craze” (PublicAffairs, 2022). During the course of writing her book, she and her sources uncovered who they believe was behind the biggest whodunnit in crypto — the 2016 DAO attack on Ethereum. She is now working on a book about the collapse of FTX. Formerly a senior editor at Forbes, she was the first mainstream journalist to cover crypto full-time, and her podcasts and videos have had 25 million downloads and views. Shin has spoken about cryptocurrency at places such as TEDx San Francisco, the International Monetary Fund, Singularity University and the Oslo Freedom Forum. She graduated Phi Beta Kappa with Honors from Stanford University and has a master of arts from Columbia University’s School of Journalism.

21.bio: Alex Pruden is the chairman of Aleo and has worked at Andreessen Horowitz and Coinbase. He graduated from Stanford University.

22.bio: Jan Liphardt is the founder of OpenMind and an associate professor of bioengineering at Stanford University.

23.bio: Daniel Vogel is the co-founder and CEO of Bitso. He holds a BA in Economics and a BS in Computer Systems Engineering from Stanford, as well as an MBA from Harvard Business School.

24.bio: Ivan Liljeqvist is an international speaker, blockchain educator, software developer, and data scientist. His YouTube channel, "Ivan on Tech," is a global phenomenon, spreading knowledge about blockchain technology to hundreds of thousands of viewers worldwide. Ivan makes the complex understandable by delivering entertaining and exciting talks. Attend one of Ivan's talks and you're sure to have a great time!

27.bio: Dr. Nicolas Kokkalis is a lifelong technologist and the Founder and Head of Technology at Pi Network. He has extensive experience in blockchain technology and software that enables large online groups to achieve their goals. In his early Ph.D. work, he developed a framework for writing "smart contracts" on fault tolerant distributed systems before blockchain and Ethereum were created. As part of his postdoc, he also introduced and taught CS359B, the Decentralized Applications on Blockchain class, at Stanford's Computer Science department.

28.bio: Dr. Chengdiao Fan brings her expertise in human behavior, social computing, and human-computer interaction to her role as Founder and Head of Product of Pi Network. She holds a Ph.D. in Anthropological Sciences from Stanford University, and her previous work includes designing software systems to improve productivity, scaling social communications, and uncovering untapped social capital for consumers.

29.bio: Ali Hindy is a Founding Member of OpenMind and previously worked as a software engineer at Microsoft, where he had extensive experience with machine learning and quantum computing projects. He is a graduate of Stanford University.

30.bio: Juan Benet is the Founder and CEO, and the inventor of InterPlanetary File System (IPFS). He is also the Founder of Filecoin, a cryptocurrency-incentivized storage network. He holds a Master of Science in Computer Science from Stanford University.

32.bio: Yilong Li is a co-founder of MegaETH and was previously a Senior Software Engineer at Runtime Verification. He has a PhD in computer science from Stanford University.

35.bio: Lyron Co Ting Keh is the founder of Seismic and previously a research fellow at the Stanford Artificial Intelligence Laboratory. He has a master's degree in computer science from Stanford University.

36.bio: Christy Choi was the founding member of Binance Labs. She is currently building Spacebar, a community-owned network to hyperconnect across DeFi, NFTs, DAOs, and game guilds.

37.bio: Chase is an avid tech enthusiast passionate about enabling new technologies such as AI and blockchain to positively impact people's lives. Before founding Crusoe, Chase was a General Partner at Polychain Capital, a $1Bn fund investing in blockchain technologies, digital assets, and energy-intensive computing applications. Chase was formerly a quantitative researcher and trader at Jump Trading and GETCO, where he developed and managed a portfolio of algorithmic trading strategies. Chase holds undergraduate degrees in mathematics and physics from MIT and a master's degree in computer science from Stanford, where he specialized in artificial intelligence. As a personal interest, Chase has climbed five of the "seven summits" including Mount Everest.

38.bio: Teresa G. is Tempo's Chief of Staff, formerly Paradigm's Head of Business Operations. He is a Stanford University graduate.

39.bio: Nikil Viswanathan is Co-Founder and CEO of Alchemy, the world's leading blockchain developer platform. He was honored as Forbes 30 Under 30, earned his BS and MS in Computer Science from Stanford, and previously held product management roles at Google, Microsoft, and Facebook. He is a serial entrepreneur and co-created the #1 social app in the world, Down To Lunch, which was featured in international media such as a New York Times business cover story. He has been featured in numerous global media outlets, including the New York Times, Forbes, NBC, Business Insider, and TechCrunch.

40.bio: Dana Hou was previously an investment partner at YZi Labs. He has also worked at Alibaba, Kuaishou, and is also a macro strategist at Goldman Sachs. Dana graduated from Peking University and holds an MBA from Stanford Graduate School of Business.

41.bio:
Entrepreneur and active investor in public and private companies. Co-Founder & CEO of Digital Assets Data.
I build and invest in companies. For fun, I play poker and run ultramarathons.

42.bio: Simon Jones is co-founder and CEO of Reya Network. He is a graduate of Stanford University and Newcastle University in the United Kingdom.

43.bio: Roham Gharegozlou is the Founder and CEO of Dapper Labs, the company behind CryptoKitties and upcoming projects such as NBA Top Shot and the Flow blockchain. He was formerly the CEO of Axiom Zen, an award-winning venture studio based in Vancouver, BC, that created ZenHub, Routific, Timeline, and more. He is an investor and advisor to several great startups, was a former VC, and is an unwavering optimist. His interests are focused on entrepreneurship, technology, bio-medicine, and economic development.

44.bio: Patrick O'Grady is the founder of commonware and was previously VP of Engineering at Avalanche and a software engineer at Coinbase. He holds a bachelor's degree in Computer science from Stanford University.

45.bio:
Ali Yahya is a partner on the a16z crypto investment team. Previously, he was a software engineer and machine learning researcher at GoogleX and Google Brain, where he worked on learning algorithms for robotic manipulation, and developed Google's open source machine learning library, TensorFlow.

Ali discovered the Bitcoin whitepaper as a computer science undergraduate at Stanford University. He became fascinated by the computer science implications and went down the rabbit hole, but somehow missed the economic implications until a few years later, and so sadly did not mine any from his dorm room. While at Stanford, Ali conducted research with Professors David Mazieres, Nick McKeown, and David Cheriton at the intersection of computer security, networking, and distributed systems.

Fun fact: For three months in 2008, Ali followed a polyphasic sleep schedule, sleeping an average of 2.5 hours a night by taking a twenty-minute nap every four hours.

46.bio: Nelson Perla-Ward is the Director of Strategic Finance at Polymarket and previously was a strategic finance Partner at a16z. He graduated from Stanford University.

47.bio: Pooja Shah is Head of Product at Rain and Founder of Fern and Radius. Previously she was head of product at Protocol Labs. She holds a BA in Computer Science from Stanford University.

48.bio: Joseph Lau is Co-Founder and CTO of Alchemy. A serial entrepreneur, he cofounded the #1 social app in the world and has been featured in international media including a New York Times Business cover story, Forbes, Business Insider, and TechCrunch. He has been honored as Forbes 30 under 30, formerly did engineering at Pinterest, Google, and Linkedin. Joe graduated with both an undergraduate and master's degree in computer science from Stanford University.

49.bio: Alex Atallah was previously the Co-founder and CTO of OpenSea. He was also the CTO of hostess.fm, which was acquired by Beatport in 2014. Prior to that, he worked at Palantir, building cybersecurity products. He graduated from Stanford University with a degree in Computer Science.

50.bio: Charles Naut is co-founder and CTO of Rain and previously worked at Lightspeed Venture. He graduated from Stanford University in the United States.

51.bio: Max Wolff is the Senior Software Engineer at Superstate. , formerly Software Engineer at Compound and Scroll, Co-founder of Marble Protocol. Economics at Stanford.

52.bio: Parker Jou is the Co-founder of Caldera.He graduated from Stanford University.

54.bio: Do Kwon is Co-Founder and CEO of Terra and was named to Forbes 30 under 30. He was formerly Founder and CEO of Anyfi, and worked at Microsoft and Apple.

56.bio: Henri Stern is the Co-founder and CEO of Privy. Previously, he was a research scientist at Protocol Labs and worked on the consensus protocol for Filecoin.

57.bio: Silas Boyd-Wickizer is the co-founder and Chief Operating Officer of Divvi. Previously, he served as Chief Technology Officer at Valora and Senior Director of Engineering at GoDaddy. Earlier in his career, he worked as a research intern at Microsoft. Silas graduated from Stanford University and holds a Ph.D. in Computer Science from the Massachusetts Institute of Technology.

58.bio: Driss Benamour is the Co-Founder & CEO of YO. Previously, he worked as Head of FinTech Products at Uber. He holds an MBA from Stanford University Graduate School of Business.

59.bio: Will Nuelle is a General Partner at Galaxy Ventures, focusing on early-stage investments in protocol layer infrastructure, DeFi applications, and software products. He began his career at Galaxy in Research, where he developed quantitative risk software for trading. Prior to Galaxy, he interned at FOAM and built incentive simulations for the Ethereum protocol. He holds B.S. degrees from Stanford University in Mathematics and Architecture. Furthermore, he is a Board Member for Skolem Technologies and has led more than 13 investments for Galaxy.

60.bio: John Guibas is the co-founder of Succinct Labs. He graduated from Stanford University in the United States.

61.bio: Shaaran Lakshminarayanan is the founder of Multipli and previously of Krypto. He graduated from Stanford University.

62.bio: Ivan Bogatyy is an engineer at Polymarket. Previously, he was Vice President of Engineering at Matter Labs, Research Partner at Dragonfly, General Partner at MetaStable, and Vice President of Engineering at zkSync. He is a graduate of Stanford University.

63.bio: E. Dean Tribble is the Co-founder and CEO of Agoric. He was previously the Principal Architect at Microsoft, and he graduated from Stanford University.

64.bio: Rajiv K. is the co-founder, president, and CEO of Auradine. He holds a bachelor's degree in computer science from the Indian Institute of Technology, Delhi, a PhD from New York University, an MSc in computer science, and an MBA from Stanford University.

65.bio: Kingsley Advani is the Founder & CEO at Allo, investor at Chainfund Capital.

66.bio: Carlos Domingo is the Founder and Managing Partner of SPiCE Venture Capital, the first tokenized VC on the blockchain that is truly liquid, inclusive, and transparent. He is also the Founder and CEO of Securitize, the leading compliance platform for security tokens or digital securities on the blockchain. Additionally, he is the former CEO of Telefonica R&D and CEO of New Business and Innovation at Telefonica Digital. Carlos is a senior executive on innovation and digital transformation, an entrepreneur, and a prolific startup investor. He is also the author of two books.

67.bio: Will Papper is Co-founder of the Syndicate Protocol. He was previously a Product Manager at Proxy and has been passionate about Ethereum since the 2014 presale. He tweets about DAOs, smart contracts, and VCs, focusing on the impact that technology has on society. He is also the Founder of Librarian AI and a Philosophy major at Stanford.

68.bio: Bobby Lee is the co-founder of BTCC and Ballet. He is the younger brother of the founder of Litecoin. Before BTCC, Mr. Lee was the Vice President of Technology for Walmart's e-commerce business in China. He graduated from Stanford University with a B.S. and an M.S. in Computer Science, and was a member of the Mayfield Fellows Program. He also earned an EMBA degree from CEIBS in China.

69.bio: Krish Chelikavada is the Co-Founder and CEO of 0xPass, a Venture Capital Fellow of DECODE, a Course Assistant at Stanford University, a Graduate Research Assistant at Stanford, a Product Manager at Microsoft, and a Software Engineer at Oracle. He holds a Master's degree in Management Science and Engineering from Stanford and a Bachelor's degree in Computer Science and Business.

70.bio: Ben El-Baz is the Head of Ecosystem at HashKey Group. Ben has over 10 years of experience in open source and business development in both Silicon Valley and China. He is a recognized thought leader and trainer in blockchain technology, the co-founder of Stanford University’s largest blockchain community, and a Stanford GSB alumnus. Ben holds a master’s degree in management from Stanford university and a bachelor’s degree in economics from Tulane University.

71.bio: Rebecca Rettig is Chief Legal Officer at Jito and previously was Chief Legal and Policy Officer at Polygon and general counsel at Aave Companies. She also works with policymakers around the world on regulatory matters related to decentralized finance (" DeFi ") and crypto assets.

72.bio: Bill Barhydt is the co-founder and CEO of Abra, a new digital cash payment app network that enables the transfer of digital assets between any two smartphones. He studied computer science at Stevens Institute of Technology and completed graduate programs in mathematics at Stanford University and finance at New York University's Stern School of Business. He previously worked at Netscape, focusing on telecommunications and online banking transactions in Europe, and later founded WebSentric Technologies, which still exists as an online portal service for SAP. He has also worked at the CIA, NASA, and Goldman Sachs.

74.bio: Sandeep Chinchali is the Chief AI Officer at Story Protocol and the Chief Scientist at Poseidon. He will lead Story's AI strategy, spanning research, product, and ecosystem development, including the launch of new primitives for decentralized data and training. He is a professor at the University of Texas at Austin where his research interests in generative artificial intelligence and network intelligence. Ph.D. in computer science from Stanford University.

75.bio: Yubo Ruan is the founder at Parallel Finance and ParaSpace. Yubo is a two-time Stanford University dropout, having studied Computer Science and Economics. He was awarded the "30 under 30" by the LA Post and the Hurun US Innovation Outstanding Award.

76.bio: Rich Heley is Chief Device Officer at Worldcoin and previously served as Head of Product integrity at Meta, where he was also Director of engineering at Apple. Rich Heley is an experienced engineering and manufacturing leader with extensive experience in consumer electronics and electric vehicles. He graduated from Rennes Institute of Technology and holds a master's degree from Stanford University.

78.bio: Guillermo Acu?a is the founder of Koywe and an entrepreneur from Chile.

79.bio: Stephen Hess is the co-founder and CEO of Metaplex. Previously, he was Head of Product at Solana. Stephen graduated from Stanford University in the United States.

80.bio: Maika Isogawa is the founder and CEO of Webacy, a venture-backed protective layer for self-custody. Prior to this, she was a cybersecurity engineer for Microsoft and was recognized as a "Forbes 30 Under 30" lister. She attended Stanford University and specialized in artificial intelligence.

81.bio: Yuchen J. is the co-founder of OpenLayer and previously was a senior software engineer and technical lead at Robinhood. He is a graduate of the University of California, Berkeley and Stanford University.

82.bio: Pankaj Gupta is the co-founder and CEO of Yupp and a former advisor to Coinbase. He graduated from the Indian Institute of Technology Delhi and holds a PhD in Computer Science from Stanford University.

83.bio: Jane He is the Chief Business Officer of AlterMe AI and previously the Executive Director of CTH, Fundamental Labs, and IDEG. She previously worked at jpmorgan Chase. She graduated from Shanghai Jiao Tong University and received a master's degree from the London School of Economics and Political Science in the United Kingdom.

84.bio: Roneil Rumburg is Co-Founder and CEO of Audius. He most recently co-founded Kleiner Perkins' early-stage seed fund, where he was responsible for seed investments into Blockchain and AI companies, including Lightning Labs. Roneil attended Stanford University and previously co-founded a Bitcoin peer-to-peer payment company called Backslash.

86.bio: Mandy Campbell is the CMO of Sharplink and previously worked at Bain Capital, OKX, Github, Facebook. She graduated from Stanford University in the United States.

87.bio: Mitch Liu is the co-founder and CEO of Theta Labs and THETA.tv. In 2010, Mr. Liu co-founded Gameview Studios, best known for its Tap Fish mobile game franchise with nearly 100 Million downloads. The company was acquired by DeNA, a leading Japanese mobile gaming company, within six months of launch. Prior to that, he co-founded Tapjoy in 2007, a pioneer of rewarded social and mobile video advertising, and grew that company to $100MM in revenues. He holds a BS in Computer Science & Engineering from MIT and an MBA from Stanford Graduate School of Business.

88.bio: Alexander Leishman is the Founder, Chief Executive Officer, and Chief Technology Officer at River Financial. He brings his expertise in software engineering, information security, and Bitcoin to River, where he is responsible for investor relations, corporate strategy, and engineering. Prior to co-founding River Financial, Alexander worked on the investment and engineering teams at Polychain Capital and Polychain Labs, focusing on Bitcoin-related venture investments. He has also served as an engineer at Airbnb's security team, a management consultant for Deloitte, and was an early engineer at Maicoin, Taiwan's largest digital asset exchange. Mr. Leishman holds a degree in Aerospace Engineering from the University of Maryland and a Master's in Computer Science from Stanford University, where he assisted in teaching the first Bitcoin class under Professor Dan Boneh.

89.bio: Matt Katz is the co-founder and CEO of Caldera and graduated from Stanford University. He has interned in software development positions at Skiff, Fractal, and Warp.

90.bio: Nemil Dalal is a Y Combinator visiting partner, former product manager at Coinbase, blogger, developer and hacker. He graduated from Stanford University with an electrical engineering major and is a Y Combinator alumnus. He has extensive experience in financial services and products, having worked at leading financial institutions in the United States, and has extensive technical expertise and management experience. He is willing to participate in early-stage venture capital investments in crypto and blockchain entrepreneurial projects, focusing on Bitcoin, blockchain and Ethereum smart contracts.

92.bio: Ukeme Okuku is the co-founder and CEO of Gamic, and previously the head of product design at Bird.Money. He is a graduate of Stanford University.

94.bio: Julia Duzon is the Chief Operating Officer at River Financial. She brings her extensive experience in product strategy and business operations in banking to River, where she oversees business development, operations, marketing, and sales. Before joining River, Julia was the Senior Director of Sales, Strategy, and Business Operations at First Republic Bank. She also worked as a Strategy and Operations Consultant at Deloitte, providing U.S. banking clients with services related to bank strategy, operations, and risk management. Ms. Duzon holds an MBA from Stanford University Graduate School of Business and a Bachelor's in Business Administration from Duke University.

97.bio: Daniel ‌Marin is the founder and CEO of Nexus Labs and previously worked at Google. He is a graduate of Stanford University and Monterey Technologies.

98.bio: Anjney Midha is currently the  General Partner at Andreessen Horowitz.Previously he worked at Discord. He graduated from Stanford University.

99.bio: Chris Burniske is the Partner of Placeholder VC, co-author of Cryptoassets, formerly led crypto ARKinvest.

100.bio: Ryan Cao is the CFO/CTO at Modulus Labs.He holds bs& ms degree from Stanford University.

101.bio: Shamal Ranasinghe is the Chief Business Officer at Audius. He is a graduate of Stanford University and Northwestern University.

102.bio: Arthur Meng is co-founder and CEO of Hemera and a venture partner at Amino Capital. He holds a PhD in biophysics from Stanford University and has published papers as first author in Nature, Nature Communications and other journals.

103.bio: JP Singh is a renowned expert in scalable infrastructure and applications. He earned his PhD from Stanford and is a Computer Science Professor at Princeton. JP is the co-director of Princeton's DeCenter, a center for blockchain technology. He was the interim CTO of Right Media, Inc., a SaaS online advertising exchange that was acquired by Yahoo! in 2007 for $850M. Prior to that, JP co-founded CaaStle, Inc., a technology and logistics firm that is revolutionizing the apparel industry with its Clothing as a Service model. He sits on the board of directors of 8x8, Inc., a publicly listed company, where he is currently the Chairman.

105.bio: Omar Siddiqui is the Founder and CEO of Joyride Games. He was previously the CEO at Kiwi, Inc. Omar Siddiqui graduated from Harvard University and Stanford University's Graduate School of Business.

106.bio: Benedikt Bunz is the co-founder and chief scientist of Espresso Systems. He is a graduate of Stanford University and the University of Zurich.

107.bio: Jesse Clayburgh has been with Protocol Labs since August 2016. Previously, he worked as a software engineer at American Express. He graduated from Stanford University.

109.bio: Rodri Fernandez Touza is the co-founder of Crossmint and previously worked at McKinsey & Company. He is a graduate of Stanford Graduate School of Business, Harvard Kennedy School, and the University of Navarra.

110.bio: Alan Chiu is currently the Co-Founder at Boba Network. He graduated from Stanford University.

112.bio: Chad Partridge is the chief AI Officer at 375ai. He is a graduate of Stanford University, the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign and the University of Michigan.

113.bio: Orion Parrott is currently the Fund General Partner at Orange DAO and the Advisor at OtoCo. He graduated from Berkeley University of California and Stanford University.

114.bio: Shuo Shan is the co-founder and Chief Technology Officer of W3W and previously was Engineering Manager at DataVisor. He received a Bachelor's degree in Engineering from Tsinghua University and a Master's degree in Engineering from Stanford University.

115.bio: David Pekar, Co-Founder and CEO at Rare Bits. At Rare Bits (rarebits.io), we believe digital goods that live on the blockchain are fundamentally better for consumers. We’re building products that introduce these goods to mainstream audiences. Formerly, Product Manager, AdMob at Google; Co-Founder, Head of Product at Toro.

116.bio: Guillaume Poncin is the director of engineering at Alchemy, a Web3 developer platform. He previously served as the head of Web3 at Stripe. During his work at Stripe, Guillaume Poncin launched a fiat-to-cryptocurrency channel and a cross-border payment product using stablecoins. Before joining Stripe, Poncin spent 12 years at Google as a technical lead for Google Books and Google Cloud. Guillaume Poncin graduated from Stanford University.

117.bio: Marc Vanlerberghe is the chief marketing officer of Algorand and previously served as senior marketing director at Google. He graduated from Stanford Graduate School of Business.

119.bio: Sophia Dew works on developer acceleration at the Ethereum Foundation, and was previously the head of developer relations at Celo and the technical lead at Gitcoin. He graduated from Stanford University.

120.bio: Andy Bromberg is the Co-founder of CoinList, a platform for deeply vetted tokens to run their ICOs. CoinList also provides compliance services for many other token sales. Previously, he was the Co-founder and CEO of Sidewire, and before that, he studied Mathematics and Computer Science at Stanford University. While there, he also co-founded the Stanford Bitcoin Group.

122.bio: Zhifeng Koh is the Head of Product at Authentickator. Presively he was Product Lead at Menyala. Zhifeng Koh graduated from Stanford.

123.bio: Romil Verma is the co-founder of Outdefine and previously worked as a software engineer at Google. He is a graduate of Stanford University and Purdue University.

124.bio: Avichal Garg is an entrepreneur and co-founder of Electric Capital. He was the Director of Product Management at Facebook, where he led the Local product team.

125.bio: Francisco Algaba de la Vieja is the Co-Founder & CEO at Giza. He also is the Guild Lead at Polygon, Core Team at W3B Lab Community. Presively, he worked as MLOps Tech Lead at adidas. Francisco Algaba graduated from Stanford University.

126.bio: Varun Kumar is the co-founder and CEO of Hashflow and a co-founder of Hibachi. He was introduced to cryptocurrency through a friend he met at HotHub. Before entering the crypto space, he worked in the aerospace industry.

127.bio: Lule Demmissie is currently the US CEO at eToro.Previously she worked at Morgan Stanley. She graduated from Stanford University and Columbia University.

128.bio: Seçkin Çağlın is a Co-Founder at Cenoa, and a former Chief Marketing & Digital Officer at Garanti BBVA Payment Systems, Engagement Manager at McKinsey & Company, and Stanford MBA graduate. He has more than 10 years of experience in product and business unit management, growth, digital product management, data & advanced analytics, brand management, marketing communications, and performance marketing.

129.bio: Emre Ertan is currently the Co-Founder of Cenoa, a simple and global super wallet to buy USD and earn high yields. Prior to this, he was the Chief Product Officer at Getir, a startup building a mobility platform. He also has experience leading growth and user acquisition, with an annual budget of up to $3.5M at Slice Technologies, Inc. and Unroll.me. Emre holds a graduate degree from Stanford University Graduate School of Business.

130.bio: Andrew Milich is co-founder and CEO of Skiff and a graduate of Stanford University.

131.bio: Rebecca Liao is the Co-Founder and CEO of Saga. She is also a Co-Founder, Advisor, and former COO of Skuchain. She is currently an Advisor to Sommelier Protocol, designing their DAO, and a Fellow at the Stanford Program in Law, Science, and Technology, where she focuses on technology policy.

132.bio: Marcus McLaughlin is the head of engineering at Caliza. He graduated from Stanford University in the United States.

133.bio: Danny Zuckerman is the founder of Ceramic Network and the head of product at Recall. He previously worked at ConsenSys. He graduated from Stanford University.

134.bio: Brandon Truong is currently the Chief Product Officer at ZetaChain. He graduated from Stanford University.

135.bio: Roel Nuyts is VP of Products and AI at Nillion, a technical advisor at Evidium-Contract, and a managing partner at Spirit River Atlas . He has continued his studies in AI at Stanford University and studied the fundamentals of quantum computing at MIT where he received a certificate.

136.bio:
Fraser Brown is the Co-Founder and CTO at Cubist. He is also an Assistant Professor at Carnegie Mellon University's School of Computer Science (Software and Societal Systems Department). His research focuses on security and program correctness, from verifying parts of production systems to automatically finding exploitable bugs in real codebases. For example, his tools have found many zero-day bountied bugs and CVEs in the popular Chrome and Firefox browsers.

Fraser also works on compilation. In collaboration with CEO Riad and others, he developed the CirC compiler for cryptographic proof systems. He received his BA in English and MS and PhD in Computer Science from Stanford, where he was supported by an NSF Graduate Research Fellowship.

137.bio: Isil Dillig is the Co-Founder & President at Veridise.She is a Professor of Computer Science at The University of Texas at Austin.

138.bio: Rafael Cosman is the Co-Founder of Archblock and TrueFi. Presively, he worked as Machine Learning Engineer at Google.

139.bio: Peter Pong is Head of engineering at Nest Wallet and was previously co-founder and CTO at Lumina. He graduated from Stanford University.

140.bio: Shruthi Rao is the Co-Founder and Chief Business Officer at Vendia. She was a Forbes Business Council Contributor and Founder & Head of Business Development at Blockchain on AWS. Shruthi Rao holds a degree from Stanford Graduate School of Business.

141.bio: Avery Haskell is a founding member and CXO of The Arena, co-founder of Park Ave Ventures, and has previously founded several companies. He graduated from Stanford University.

142.bio: Xochitl Cazador is Chief of Staff and Head of Finance and Business Operations at Optimism. She was previously Chief Ecosystem Growth Officer at the Celo Foundation. She is a Stanford University graduate.

143.bio: Jason Ginsberg is co-founder and CTO of Skiff and was previously a software engineer at Apple and Visby.io.

144.bio: Rachin Kalakheti is the founder of Cedro Finance. He is currently pursuing a Bachelor of Science degree in Computer Science at Stanford University.

146.bio: Alana Ackerson is President of Oasis Pro and previously CEO of HQ Digital, part of DCG. She holds a bachelor's degree from Stanford University.

147.bio: Jerry Liu is the founder of Euterpe and Fellow of the Center for Internet and Society of Stanford Law School. He obtained his J.S.D. degree at Stanford Law School.

148.bio: Alessio Quaglini is the CEO and Co-Founder of Hex Trust and Clearpool. He holds a degree in Quantitative Finance from Stanford University and an MBA from UCLA.

149.bio: Jeff Boortz is Blockstream's Chief Product Officer, responsible for managing the Blockstream product portfolio, and has more than 20 years of product management and IT experience. He holds an MBA from the University of California, Berkeley and a BS in Industrial Engineering from Stanford University.

150.bio: Soravis Srinawakoon is the CEO of Band Protocol and was featured in Forbes 30 under 30. He previously worked as a management consultant with a strong background in computer science and has a deep understanding of the crypto industry. He is proficient with analytic tools and data visualization software and has experience in the consumer goods, retail, and creative industries.

151.bio: Ahmed Attia is the founder of Synquote and previously worked as a software engineer at Google and IDEX. He graduated from Stanford University in the United States.

152.bio: Nnamso Anthony is co-founder, COO, and VP of Engineering at Gamic. He graduated from Stanford University.

153.bio: Ricky Wang works on investments at YZi Labs, and is a former Director of the Stanford Blockchain Accelerator, Head of BD at Cyber, a Former Pro of Clash Royale (2017-2018), and host of the podcast The Story of Web3. He has a B.S. from the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign's Geis School of Business.

154.bio: Chester Szeen is the Head of Growth at AVA Foundation and was previously the COO of Seedify.fund. He graduated from the University of Rochester, Stanford Graduate School of Business, and the Chinese University of Hong Kong.

156.bio: Marcel Fohrmann is the Co-Founder at Subsquid

157.bio: Anshum Bhambri is currently the Founder at FanCraze. He graduated from Stanford University and University of Chicago.

159.bio: Mario Eguiluz Alebicto is the founder and CTO of Deblock and previously was Head of Engineering at Ledger. He graduated from Stanford University and Deusto University in the United States.

160.bio: Daniel Shorr is the Co-founder & CEO at Modulus Labs.He holds bs& ms degree from Stanford University.

161.bio: Ben Livshits is an advisor at Brave, and previously served as CTO of Eclipse, scientific advisor for FailSafe, and CEO of Zilliqa. He is also an associate professor at Imperial College London, holds a PhD from Stanford University, and previously worked as a research scientist at Microsoft Research. He graduated from Cornell University and Stanford University, has authored hundreds of academic papers, and holds dozens of patents.

162.bio: Dennis J.H. Won is a Technical Partner at L2 Iterative Ventures and a member of Magic's technical staff. Prior to that he was a founding member and core protocol lead at Harmony Protocol and a technical director at Kaia.Dennis is a graduate of Stanford University.

163.bio: Deian Stefan is the Co-Founder and Chief Scientist at Cubist. He is also an Associate Professor of Computer Science and Engineering at UC San Diego, where he co-leads the Security and Programming Systems groups. His research focuses on the intersection of security and programming languages. He is particularly interested in constructing secure systems that are deployed in production. He was a co-founder of Intrinsic, a runtime security startup which was acquired by VMware in 2019.

164.bio: Adam Goldberg is a Managing Director and Co-Founder of Standard Crypto, a venture capital firm that has been fortunate to partner with Aave, Sommelier, Telegram, Bitmain, Matrixport, OpenSea, Foundation, Instadapp, and many other companies and crypto-networks. He previously led blockchain and crypto investing at Lightspeed Venture Partners.

165.bio: Riad Wahby is the Co-Founder and CEO of Cubist. Previously, he was a cryptographic researcher at Algorand and spent a decade as an analog and mixed-signal integrated circuit designer at Silicon Labs. He received his SB and MEng in Electrical Engineering and Computer Science from MIT and his PhD in Computer Science from Stanford, where he was supported by a Ripple Fellowship.

166.bio: Griffin Dunaif is the co-founder of Halliday.

167.bio: Poramin Insom is Founder and Core Developer at Firo. Experienced Founder with a demonstrated history of working in the computer and network security industry. He graduated from The Johns Hopkins University.

168.bio: Michael Arrington is the Founder of TechCrunch, CrunchBase and Arrington Capital.

169.bio: Esteban Castaño is the CEO of TRM Labs. He holds an MBA from the Stanford University Graduate School of Business.

170.bio: Shaun is a Partner at Sequoia Capital. Previously he Co-Founded Expanse which was acquired by Palo Alto Networks for $800M. He was also a Partner at GV. He has a Ph.D. in Physics from Caltech and Masters's degree in Statistics from Stanford and a "Control and Dynamical Systems" from Caltech.

172.bio: Sam Forman is the co-founder of Sturdy and the co-founder and CEO of TaoFi. He holds a bachelor's and master's degree in computer science from Stanford University.

173.bio: Ryne Saxe is the Co-founder and Chief Operating Officer of Eco. He graduated from Stanford Law School.

174.bio: Sveinn Valfells is the Co-founder and CEO of Monerium. He holds a Bachelor of Science in Applied Physics from Columbia University (1987-1990), a Doctor of Philosophy in Physics from Boston University (1991-1996), and a Master of Science in Management from Stanford University (1997-1998).

175.bio: Dan Tranimal is Head of Business at NFTGo and holds a bachelor's degree in economics from Stanford University.

176.bio: Alok Vasudev is Co-Founder of Standard Crypto, Early-stage technology investor. Formerly, Partner at s28 Capital.

178.bio: Dima Kogan is the co-founder and CTO of Fordefi and previously worked at Google. He is a graduate of Stanford University and the Hebrew University of Jerusalem.

180.bio: Nilmini Rubin is the Chief Policy Officer of Hedera and has previously served as the Public Policy Manager at Meta. She graduated from Yale University.

181.bio:
CEO of BlockCypher, Engineer, formerly US Naval Officer. Mother, STEM advocate for girls.
15+ years in software product management, product marketing, environmental & energy, and defense; 5+ years as a U.S. Naval Officer.
Specialties: start-ups in crypto-currency, CRM, energy management systems, product management, product marketing, investor relations, environmental management.

182.bio: Saalim Chowdhury is a Senior Advisor at ETFules.

183.bio: Zenobia Godschalk is the Senior Vice President at Hedera.

184.bio: Peiqian Li is co-founder and co-chief technology officer of Aperture Finance and was previously a senior software engineer at Google. He holds a master's degree in computer science from Stanford University.

185.bio: Elodie de Marchi-Chouard  is the Chief Operating Officer at Kaiko. Presively she was Mentor at LBAN - Stanford Latino Entrepreneurship Initiative. worked at Silicon Valley Bank. Elodie de Marchi graduated from Stanford University.

186.bio:
Danny Greene is the Head of Community at Linera. Prior to this role, he served as the former Meebits Brand Lead at Yuga Labs, where he managed an 11-person cross-functional team responsible for business development, product development, and strategic communications.

Danny Greene holds a bachelor's degree from Stanford University.

187.bio: Mike O'Sullivan is co-founder and CTO of aZen Protocol, specializing in the development of intelligent systems using operations research (OR) and analytics. He attended Stanford University for both his master's and doctoral degrees.

189.bio: Juan Pablo Lafosse is the Co-Founder and CEO of TravelX. He is also a partner investor of Yavu Ventures. Juan graduated from Stanford University.

190.bio: Erik Svenson is the co-founder, President, and CFO of Blockstream. He has more than 20 years of experience in leading strategies, operations, and fundraising in his previous roles. He spent 10 years in the traditional finance industry in leading roles, followed by another decade as a co-founder and consultant for rapidly growing technology startups. Erik has a BA from Stanford and is a former CFA charterholder.

191.bio: William Croisettier is the Chief Growth Officer at zkCandy and previously served as Head of Ecosystem Venture Capital at Parity Technologies and R3. He is a graduate of Stanford University.

192.bio: David Lawant is the head of research at Anchorage, and he previously worked at FalconX, Bitwise, Hashdex. He graduated from Stanford Graduate School of Business with a master's degree.

193.bio:
Guy Wuollet is a partner on the a16z crypto investment team. Guy focuses on infrastructure and application layer investments across crypto. He believes decentralized networks can better align incentives on the Internet and enable the future of commerce and communication.
Prior to joining a16z, he worked on independent research in concert with Protocol Labs. His work focused on building decentralized networking protocols and upgrading Internet infrastructure. Guy holds a B.S. in Computer Science from Stanford University, where he rowed on the Varsity Crew team.

194.bio: Georgi Koreli is the Co-Founder & CEO at Hinkal Protocol. He graduated from Stanford University Graduate School of Business.

195.bio: Freeman Fan is the founder and CEO of Earth From Another Sun. He is a graduate of Stanford University and the University of Alberta in Canada.

196.bio: Prabhdeep Chawla is the Director of Partnerships at Abound. He holds a Masters in Management from Stanford University.

197.bio: 공윤진 is the co-founder and CTO of GOPAX. He holds a Bachelor of Science in Computer Science/Mathematics from Stanford University and a Master of Science in Computer Science from Stanford University. He previously worked as an engineer at Google.

198.bio: Anton Derlyatka is the co-founder of Sweatcoin.

199.bio: Aaron Greenblatt was previously the founder and CEO of Turbo Protocol and formerly the founder and CEO of IronMill. He graduated from Stanford University and Tufts University.

200.bio: Yanfeng Wang is the Vice President of Research and Development at Bitmain. Previously he was a committee member of the International Electron Devices Conference, a senior manager at NVIDIA, and a senior scientist at IBM.Yanfeng Wang received his Bachelor's degree from Tsinghua University (B.S. in Applied Physics), Master's degree from Stanford University (Project Management), and Ph.D. from Pennsylvania State University (Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering).

201.bio: Manav Aggarwal is the product team leader at Celestia and was previously a software engineer at Two Sigma. He is a graduate of Stanford University.

202.bio: Akbar Thobhani is the Founder and CEO of SFOX. He is a graduate of Stanford University and holds an MBA from the Massachusetts Institute of Technology-Sloan School of Management. Prior to this, he served as Head of Business Development at Airbnb.

203.bio: Thomas France is currently the Board Advisor at Ledger and the Cofounder & GP at Cygni Capital. He graduated from Stanford University.

204.bio: Aaron Karczmer is a member of the board of directors of zerohash. He is the CEO of K2 Integrity. He is a Ph.D. graduate of Stanford Law School.

205.bio: Louis Baudoin is CEO at Merkle, the Bloomberg for blockchains. Louis worked at Mckinsey for two years, mostly focusing on risk management and asset valuation for financial institutions. Louis was involved as a PM and Data Scientist in the very early age of the e-commerce companies Linio and Lazada. Lazada is now the largest e-commerce company focused on South-East Asia and worth multiple Bn $. Louis co-wrote two reports on value chain design and Circular Economy that were presented at the World Economic Forum at Davos in 2015 and 2016. Louis hold a BSc in Fundamental Maths and a MSc in Applied Maths from a French grande école (Supaero), a MSc in Finance from the London School of Economics, and a MBA from Stanford GSB.

206.bio: João Victor Aguiar is the Co-founder of ECSA. He graduated from Stanford University's Graduate School of Business.

207.bio: Jason Rosenthal is an operating partner at Andreessen Horowit and previously served as vice president of subscription services at Google. He is a graduate of Stanford Graduate School of Business and Pomona College.

208.bio: Mohamed Elkastawi is the CEO and co-founder of Hamilton, previously a co-founder of Tribal. He holds a bachelor's degree in Engineering Management from Northern Illinois University.

209.bio: Catherine  Gu is currently the Head of Product, Digital Assets at Solana.Previously he worked at Gauntlet and Anchorage and Visa. She graduated from Stanford University and University of Cambridge.

210.bio: Vlad Tenev is the co-founder, chairman, and CEO of Robinhood, and the co-founder and executive chairman of Harmonic. He is a graduate of Stanford University and UCLA.

211.bio: Satyan Gajwani is the Chairman of Abound. He holds a B.S. in Mathematics and Computer Science and an M.S. in Management Science and Engineering from Stanford University.

213.bio: Augie is an Investment Partner and Head of Asia at CMT Digital, a crypto and blockchain investment firm based in the US. He was previously with Sequoia Capital Southeast Asia where he spearheaded their crypto investments in the region. Before Sequoia, Augie spent time at McKinsey & Company focused on emerging markets, working on projects in Southeast Asia, LATAM, and the US. He holds a BA and MS from Stanford University and was selected for Forbes 30 under 30 in 2022.

215.bio:
Neal Kaufman is the CEO of Aligned. He has extensive financial experience as a CFO, Hedge Fund Managing Director, and CEO of a public company. He was a senior executive at NBC Internet (a division of GE) and 3Com Corporation. He began his career working for McKinsey & Co. in the US, Europe, and Latin America.

Neal has an MBA from Harvard Business School, where he was a Baker Scholar, and he holds a M.A. from Stanford University. He received his bachelor's degree in Economics from Harvard College, where he earned a varsity letter in football.

216.bio: Guillermo is a Stanford lifer (BS, MS, and PhD in Electrical Engineering). He spends most of his work time in a dark corner of a room, figuring out random math problems for fun. He also loves to work with teams to model many of the weird (and fascinating) incentives in DeFi and beyond. Outside of work, Guillermo likes to cook pizza, hike, and have long, silly conversations with friends over wine.

217.bio: Tariq Patanam is the Co-founder at teablocks.Presively, he worked as Software Engineer at YouTube and Google.Tariq Patanam graduated from Stanford University.

218.bio: Gary is the Managing Partner at Momentum Capital. Gary is a Silicon Valley-based investor, a serial entrepreneur, a Stanford-affiliated researcher, and a true believer in Web3. Gary has over six years of experience in blockchain technology and is currently focusing on scaling, DID, and liquidity solutions. Gary finished his Ph.D. and postdoc research at Stanford University before transitioning into the industry.

219.bio: Katie Haun is the CEO and Founder of Haun Ventures, a $1.5 billion venture capital fund focused on web3. Previously, she was a General Partner at Andreessen Horowitz, a Silicon Valley-based venture capital firm, and co-chaired its three crypto funds. She has testified before both houses of Congress on the intersection of technology and regulation, and has taught at Stanford's GSB.

220.bio:
Annie Eckert is Head of Product at Thirdwave and was previously Head of Product Marketing at Meta. 

She is a graduate of Stanford University.

221.bio: Celeste Ang is currently the Head of Developer Relations at Virtuals Protocol and the Core-Contributor at Luna. She graduated from Stanford University.

223.bio: Nicole Liu is the Founding Partner of Comma3 Ventures. Previously, she worked at Jubilee Capital Management Pte Ltd and China Mobile Phones. Nicole Liu graduated from Stanford University and Nanyang Technological University in Singapore.

224.bio:
Zoe Weinberg is the Founder & managing partner of ex/ante. Previously, Zoe served on the National Security Commission on Artificial Intelligence and at Google AI. Prior she focused on national security, working in Mosul Iraq during the counter-ISIL operation in 2017. She was an investor at the International Finance Corporation of the World Bank and at Goldman Sachs.

Her work on national security has been published in the New York Times and Foreign Affairs and she co-hosts the podcast Next in Foreign Policy. Zoe received her JD from Yale Law School, MBA from Stanford Graduate School of Business, and BA from Harvard College.

225.bio: Curtis Spencer is a co-founder of Electric Capital, having previously co-founded Spool, a mobile content caching startup with Avichal Garg, which was acquired by Meta.

227.bio:
Elizabeth Yin is a co-founder and General Partner at Hustle Fund, a pre-seed fund for software entrepreneurs. She was previously a Partner at 500 Startups, where she invested in seed-stage companies and ran the Mountain View accelerator. Elizabeth also co-founded and ran an adtech company called LaunchBit, which was acquired in 2014. She holds a BSEE from Stanford and an MBA from MIT Sloan.

Elizabeth has reviewed over 20,000 startup pitches from around the world in the last few years and has helped numerous portfolio founders raise hundreds of millions of dollars. Her work and writing on startup fundraising has been featured in TechCrunch, Forbes, Huffington Post, BetaKit, and more.

229.bio: Sharon Henley is Head of marketing at The Open Platform and was previously chief Product Officer and Chief Marketing Officer at Coincover. She is a graduate of Stanford University and has previously worked as a marketing executive at Nikon and The Royal Mint.

231.bio: Rex Woodbury is a Partner at Index Ventures, an investment firm focused on helping entrepreneurs turn ideas into global businesses. He is also the founder of Digital Native, a platform exploring the collision of technology and humanity. Rex has also held leadership positions at Flagship, Beam Impact, and Persona. He holds a Master of Business Administration from Stanford University and a Bachelor of Arts degree in Economics from Dartmouth College.

232.bio: Lauren Gross is the COO and partner of Founders Fund and was previously an analyst at citi. She graduated from Stanford University in the United States.

233.bio: Jing Xiong is the Investment Director at YZi Labs and previously worked at Google. She holds a PhD from Stanford University.

235.bio:
Galen is a seasoned venture capital investor and founder of Double Peak Group, his personal family office. He brings deep financial industry understanding and a proven track record in managing complex investments.
Galen is also a former core member of the PwC Crypto Team. Leveraging his extensive experience in fintech and crypto, he has played a core role in driving high profile firm-wide projects and global initiatives.
Galen graduated from Hong Kong University of Science and Technology and Stanford University Graduate School of Business.

236.bio: Robin Linus is a core contributor to ZeroSync and the creator of BitVM.org.

237.bio: Tuck is the founding partner of SWC Global and has extensive experiences in early-stage to growth-stage investments in India, Southeast Asia and China. He co-founded Shunwei Capital in 2011, and has raised and managed over US$6 billion dollars in funds to invest in the technology sector. Tuck has invested across multiple sectors including social media, transactional platforms, consumer and deep technology. He was recognized by Forbes in its 2020 and 2021 “The Midas List” for being one of the top 100 venture investors globally. Tuck was also recognized by Fortune China as one of the 30 most influential investors in China in 2019, 2020 and 2021.  Tuck graduated from the National University of Singapore and Stanford University.

238.bio: Benedict Ho is the Managing Partner & CEO, Co-founder at MaiCapital. Benedict Ho has 20 years of experience overseeing investment and business operations in institutional asset management company. He oversaw Fubon Bank's pension fund and its fund business before co-founding MaiCapital. Prior to Fubon he was in charge of the trading desk at a multi-billion hedge fund with clients from Sovereign Fund and major banks in Asia. Ben holds a Master's degree in Management Science and Engineering from Stanford University and bachelor degree in Computer Engineering from University of Washington.

239.bio: Keith Rabois is an American technology executive and investor. He is the managing director of Khosla Ventures and has previously served as an investor and executive at early-stage startups in Founders Fund, PayPal, LinkedIn, Slide, and Square.

240.bio: Molly Mackinlay is the Engineering & Research Lead at Protocol Labs and was previously a product manager at Google. She graduated from Stanford University.

241.bio: Magdalena Kala is the founder of Double Down. She is passionate about consumer behavior, brand building, and mainstream adoption of web3. She graduated from Stanford University and Harvard University.

242.bio: Robert Drost is CEO and Executive Director of the Eigen Foundation, research partner at Ethereal Ventures, and previously Head of Research and development at ConsenSys Mesh. He has a PhD in electrical engineering from Stanford University.

243.bio: James Rathmell is general counsel at Haun Ventures and previously was a crypto consultant at Andreessen Horowitz. He graduated from Stanford Graduate School of Business.

244.bio: Ilya Fushman is a Partner at Kleiner Perkins, a venture capital firm based in the San Francisco Bay Area. In this role, he has also served as a board director for various companies, including Autograph, Welcome, Inkitt, and Cameo. Prior to his current position, he obtained a PhD and MS in Applied Physics and Electrical Engineering from Stanford University and a BSc in Physics from Caltech.

245.bio: Ethan Fast is co-founder and CTO of Nash, a non-custodial exchange and management platform for cryptocurrencies and other digital assets. He has a PhD in Computer Science from Stanford and has published articles in renowned journals and conferences, such as Nature, CHI, EMNLP, and AAAI. Prior to this, he co-founded Proxino, a web analytics company funded by Y Combinator.

246.bio: Hans Tung is the Managing Partner at GGV Capital and   Notable Capital.

248.bio: Noah Levine is an investment partner at a16z and was previously a cryptocurrency analyst at Visa and head of data and analytics at RTFKT. He graduated from Stanford University and the University of Southern California.

249.bio: Claudio Lima is the co-founder of Iagon. He is a graduate of the University of Kent, Stanford University and Campinas State University.

250.bio:
Grace Kwan is a co-founder of Orca and a former software engineer at Coursera.

She holds a B.S. and M.S. in Computer Science from Stanford University.

251.bio: Will McTighe is the co-founder and Head of Strategy at Web3 Analytics.

253.bio: Gil Rosen is the Managing Partner of Blockchain Builders Fund. Gil is the President of the Stanford Blockchain Accelerator, and co-teacher of CEE246A Web3 Entrepreneurship in the Stanford School of Engineering.

254.bio: Richard Sobel is a partner at Polymorphic Capital and holds a BA in Economics from Stanford University.

255.bio: Sophia Sun Zou is Investor at Greycroft. Presively she was  the Principal at Breyer Capital. Sophia leads sustainability investing for Breyer Capital. Beyond that, she led and/or supports investments in portfolio companies like AppHarvest, Charm Industrial, Taager, and Pledge. Before joining Breyer Capital, Sophia was a consultant at the Boston Consulting Group, with experience working across public policy, economic development, energy, as well as early-stage venture incubation in healthtech and insurtech. Sophia graduated with First Class Honors from the University of Sydney with a triple major in International Relations, Chinese Studies, and French Studies.

256.bio: Natalya Thakur is the Co-Founder and COO of Knox Networks, as well as Co-Founder and Advisor of the Stanford Future of Digital Currency Initiative.

257.bio: Lukas Scheucher is the CTO of Compass Labs and previously worked as a software engineer at Google. He graduated from Stanford University and the Technical University of Munich

258.bio: Felix Meng is the co-founder and CEO of XTrace. He has a master's degree in computational mathematics from Stanford University.

259.bio: Kanyi Maqubela is a Managing Partner at Kindred Ventures. He served as a Partner at Collaborative Fund, where he was an early advisor to Tala and Walker & Co., and a board member at Buffer, Camino Financial, Spruce, and True Link. Kanyi is a co-founder at Heartbeat Health, and previously ran growth at One Block Off the Grid (acquired by $NRG) and was an early employee at Doostang (acquired by Universum Global). Kanyi has also served as a Lecturer and Adjunct at New York University Tisch School of the Arts, a curriculum adapted from his time as a student at Stanford University.

260.bio: Scarlet Chen is the CEO of Cytus Protocol. She graduated from Stanford with a PhD in Economics.

261.bio: Grant Sohn is the co-founder of Carry Protocol and previously worked at McKinsey & Company. He graduated from Stanford University in the United States.

262.bio: Steven Willinger is the Partner of Blockchain Builders Fund. Presively he was Ventures Lead at Coinbase. Product Management at Blockstream. GTM at Google. Steven Willinger graduated from Stanford University Graduate School of Business.

263.bio: Serge Kassardjian is a highly experienced entrepreneur and business leader, serving as the co-founder and CEO of StayTuned Digital, a conversational messaging company. Prior to this, Serge spent time at Google, where he was the Global Head of Media Apps for Google Play and Android. During his tenure, he led global business development efforts with media companies across various verticals, including entertainment, video, sports, news, virtual reality, and cable. Serge was also a member of the launch team for Daydream Virtual Reality, Android TV, and various Nexus devices. Before joining Google, Serge co-founded NextRev Commerce, an AR and VR company that helps brands and retailers build immersive commerce solutions. He also worked as a Venture Capital Investor at Globespan Capital Partners and as a Corporate Development Director in Sony Music's Global Digital Business. Serge began his career as an investment banker at Morgan Stanley. He holds an MBA from Harvard and a Bachelor's and Master's degree in Engineering from Stanford, where he served as the President of his class.

264.bio: Scott Starrett is currently the Chief of Staff at Mad Realities. He graduated from Stanford University.

266.bio: Simon Ratner is the Co-Founder and CTO of Proxy. He graduated from Stanford University and previously worked as an engineer at YouTube.

267.bio: Grace Isford is the partner at Lux Capital. Before joining Lux, Grace was a principal at Canvas Ventures.Prior to Canvas, Grace worked on the LP side at the Stanford Management Company, in product at edtech startup Handshake, and in growth equity at Stripes Group. She earned a Bachelors of Science and a Masters of Science in Management Science and Engineering from Stanford, where she was a Mayfield Fellow and served as Co-President of Stanford Women in Business, the campus’s largest pre-professional organization for women.

268.bio: Dave Hendricks is the founder and CEO of Vertalo, which aims to build the infrastructure of digital assets, including issuance, custody and trading brokerage. In addition, he is also the co-founder of Tokenizer and LiveIntent. He graduated from Stanford University and holds a master's degree in computer science from the university and a doctorate in art from George Washington University.

269.bio: Haiyang Ru is chief risk officer at HashKey Group. He was previously deputy general manager of Alipay and senior director of Meituan. He graduated from Fudan University with a major in international finance.

270.bio: Anthony Ghosn is the founder and general partner of Pharsalus Capital and graduated from Stanford University.

271.bio: David Woodbury is currently the Co-Founder at YAK DAO. He graduated from Stanford University.

272.bio: Fernando Gouveia is the founder and Managing Partner of Compa Capital, and previously he was Head of Business Development at Xapo. He is a graduate of Santa Clara University, Stanford University and Pompeu Fabra University.

273.bio: Daniel Bulaevsky is a partner, GC and COO at Hack VC. He is a graduate of Stanford Law School and Williams College.

274.bio: Jake Greenstein is a Partner and the Head of Infrastructure at Hivemind. Jake focuses on Hivemind’s staking and mining activity, capital deployment into DeFi applications, and leads DeFi and Market Infrastructure venture investments, as well as Hivemind's internal infrastructure needs. Prior to Hivemind, Jake spent the last four years leading Algorand’s strategy team, where he supported the organization’s growth from a dozen to one hundred employees. Jake also played a key role in launching the Algorand protocol and the achievement of its all-time high market cap of over $13B. Before joining Algorand, Jake pioneered a hybrid software development and consulting role at Mastercard where he led teams building analytic and other software products for external clients and the firm’s own use. Jake received his MBA from Stanford University where he graduated as an Arjay Miller Scholar. He also received a BSE in Mechanical Engineering from Duke University and was elected to the Phi Betta Kappa and Tau Beta Pi honor societies.

275.bio: Graeme Olson is the CTO of BTC Beta. He graduated from MIT and holds a Master of Science degree in Computer Science and Information Science from Stanford University. Graeme Olson is a pioneer in computer geeks and an early developer of Bitcoin.

276.bio: Marco is an executive in the technology and investment industries, with expertise in venture capital and company-building. He has served as Managing Director at Evolution Media Partners, part of TPG Capital, and President and CEO of PlayFirst, Inc. He holds an MBA from Carnegie Mellon University, as well as degrees in Aeronautics/Astronautics and Mechanical Engineering from Stanford University, San Jose State University, and the U.S. Naval Postgraduate School. Marco has achieved success in the media and entertainment industry, having sold PlayFirst to Glu Mobile.

277.bio: Tipatat is a spokesperson and thought leader in the VR/AR industry, providing advice and mentorship to companies, incubators, and accelerators globally. With expertise in creating interactive and immersive content, he aims to advance the future of broadcasting by improving the user experience through dynamic content delivery. Tipatat is a regular contributor to publications and industry events, serves as an advisor to Tokyo VR Startups, and was the co-founder and CEO of the mobile game startup Big Head Mode, Inc. He holds a Bachelor of Science in Symbolic Systems with a concentration in Human-Computer Interaction from Stanford University.

279.bio: Vitaly Spassky is a managing partner at Polymorphic Capital, a former managing partner at G1 Ventures, and a graduate of Stanford University.

280.bio: Valentin Preobrazhenskiy is the founder of LATOKEN and was previously the founder and CEO of AIB Zalogo. He graduated from Stanford University in the United States.

281.bio: Spencer Greene is the General Partner at TSVC. He was Lead of "Design for Exit". Former Head of M&A at Juniper Networks. He graduated from Rice University and Stanford University.

282.bio: Gavi Galloway is the Lead Engineer at Standard Crypto. He graduated from Stanford University.

283.bio: Suzanne Kim is the Partner, Head of Investor Relations at Haun Ventures. She holds a MBA from Stanford University Graduate School of Business.

284.bio: Uwe Cerron is a portfolio manager at Coinchange and was previously a core contributor to Convergence. He is a graduate of Stanford University.

285.bio: Keatly Haldeman is currently the Managing Partner at Praxos Capital Management. He graduated from Stanford University and Duke University.

286.bio: Lauren DeMeuse is a partner and COO at Theory Ventures and previously worked at 8VC. She graduated from Stanford Graduate School of Business with a master's degree.

287.bio: Laurent Parmentier is a partner at Stratos, having previously been a partner at SignalFire. He is a graduate of Stanford University, London School of Economics and Political Science, and University College London.

288.bio: Jose Marin is the Founding Partner of FJ Labs.He graduated from Stanford University Graduate School of Business.

289.bio: Chhi'mèd Künzang is the CTO of Proofbase, and was previously co-founder and Chief Scientist of Argument.

290.bio: Bohua (Jack) Wang is currently the Investor at Avenir Group that focusing on US market and on AI theme.

291.bio: Ned Rockson is the Principal Software Engineer at Snowflake. He is the Co-Founder at SlashAut & Debrief. Ned Rockson graduated from Stanford University.

293.bio: Charles Srisuwananukorn is currently the AI Investment Partner at Cadenza Ventures. He graduated from Stanford University.

294.bio: LIWEN OUYANG is a co-founder of XTrace. He has a master's degree from Stanford University.

295.bio: Xavier Segura is a general partner at Morgan Creek Digital. He is a graduate of Columbia Business School, the University of Michigan, Stanford Graduate School of Business and the Massachusetts Institute of Technology.

296.bio: Michael Golomb is the CFO of Blockstream and the founder of FANVESTOR. He previously worked at BitFury. He holds a Master of Science degree from Stanford University's Graduate School of Business.

298.bio: Vincent Niu is a former partner at Sky9 Capital, focusing on investments in the blockchain industry infrastructure, middleware, application layer (DeFi/NFT) and other fields. He previously founded DappReview, a decentralized application data analysis platform, which was acquired by Binance in 2019. Since then, he has also served as the product leader of CoinMarketCap and has invested in several early NFT and DeFi projects. Prior to this, Niu Fengxuan worked as a quantitative analyst at BlackRock.

299.bio: William Robinson is a core contributor to Alliance DAO and a graduate of Concordia University.

300.bio: Khoi Le Previously he worked at Fanhouse. He graduated from Stanford University.

302.bio: Linus Chung previously was the Head of Product of Magic Eden and vice president of product at Origin Protocol and Director of Product at Coinbase.

303.bio: Yingkai Xu is the founder and CEO of Onebit, and previously served as the CEO of DeData. He graduated from Stanford University and University College London.

305.bio: Alex Kahn was the former CEO of Econia. He graduated from the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign and holds a Master of Science degree in Mechanical Engineering from Stanford University.

306.bio: Labhesh Patel was previously the CEO of Autonomys. He graduated from the Indian Institute of Technology Kanpur and Stanford University.

308.bio: Kemal El Moujahid is the former CPO at Chainlink and reviously worked as a product management manager at Google and meta. He graduated from Stanford Graduate School of Business.

309.bio: John Lilly is the Board Member at Figma.He spents the past 30 years working at and investing in technology companies that help people create & connect -- Mozilla, Apple, Instagram, Duolingo, Figma, Dropbox, Tumblr & more. He also spent a lot of time helping non-profit and civic tech organizations like Code for America, VotingWorks and Daffy.org.

310.bio: Axel Ericsson is co-founder of staking services firm Vest, 2018 Thiel Fellow.

311.bio: Arun Devabhaktuni was previously the head of research at Mirana Ventures. He graduated from MIT and holds a PhD in biology from Stanford University School of Medicine.

312.bio: Gaby Goldberg currently works in Rostra and has previously worked at TCG Crypto, Bessemer Ventures, Chapter One. He graduated from Stanford University.

313.bio: 0age currently works at Uniswap and was previously the Head of Protocol Development at OpenSea, where he oversees the protocol development of the entire OpenSea platform. He is the author of Seaport, a new open-source protocol powering NFT marketplaces like OpenSea. Prior to OpenSea, 0age was the CTO of Dharma Labs (acquired by OpenSea).

315.bio: Theo Diamandis was previously a research partner at Bain Capital Crypto. He graduated from Stanford University with a master's degree in electrical engineering and from MIT with a PhD in electrical engineering and computer science.

316.bio: Lindsay Lamont is the former head of marketing at Uniswap Labs and was previously an angel investor and manager at Jane Capital. She graduated from Stanford University in the United States.

317.bio: Harry Mellsop was previously the Chief Technology Officer of Transpose and the Engineering Director of Chainalysis. He graduated from Stanford University with a degree in Computer Science.

319.bio: Forrest Browning was previously co-founder and chief product officer of Audius. He graduated from Stanford University.

320.bio: Luke Tchang is the co-founder and CEO of Nocturne, and previously a founding team member and technical lead at Nomad. He holds a Bachelor of Science degree in Computer Science from Stanford University.

321.bio: Akash Garg is currently a consultant at Electric Capital and previously served as CTO of MoonPay and VP of Engineering at Block. He holds a master's degree in computer science from Stanford University.

322.bio: Anish Mohammed was previously the co-founder, CTO, and chief scientist of Panther Protocol, and served as the chief architect of Boson Protocol. He has also been an advisor to SMAPE Capital, Seed Club Ventures, and Ocean Protocol. He graduated from Royal Holloway, University of London, and Stanford Business School.

323.bio: Michael Zhu is a research engineer for a16z crypto, writing open-source code in collaboration with the research team and helping portfolio companies on all things smart contract-related. Prior to joining a16z crypto, Michael led protocol development at 0x Labs, architecting smart contracts to facilitate peer-to-peer trading on multiple blockchains. Before that, he was a student at Stanford University, where he received BS and MS degrees in Computer Science.

325.bio: Alexia Bonatsos is a writer, investor and startup hound, who has worked for some of the most notable media outlets, including TechCrunch, SF Weekly and LA Weekly. She currently serves as Founder and Managing Director of Dream Machine, a full-time position she has held since 2016. Alexia also volunteers as an Editor for All Raise, editing and strategizing blog content to showcase luminary female founders and funders. She holds an MBA from the Stanford Graduate School of Business, as well as a Bachelor of Arts in English Language and Literature and Fine Arts from the University of Southern California. Her skills include digital media, new media, and related fields.

326.bio: Erica Kang is the Founder and CEO of KryptoSeoul, a community building team based in Seoul, Korea. She has been actively hosting meetups for post-ICO projects not only in Seoul but also globally. She is also the organizer of BUIDL conference, a technical conference focused on projects that build and connecting them to the right players.

327.bio: Sophia Huard was previously the head of product operations at Opensea. She has served as the head of product operations security and privacy at Robinhood and a privacy and security product expert at Facebook. She graduated from Stanford University.

328.bio: Dimitri Dadiomov is the co-founder and CEO of Modern Treasury. He received his undergraduate degree from Stanford University and holds an MBA from Harvard Business School.

329.bio: Jerry Yang, born in Taipei, Republic of China, has both the nationality of the Republic of China and the United States. He is the founder and former CEO of Internet portal and search giant Yahoo. He is also a well-known angel investor. According to the statistics of "Forbes" magazine in 2005, his personal assets are 2.6 billion US dollars, ranking 228th in the world, and he holds 3.63% of Yahoo's shares.

331.bio: Liz Chien previously served as Global Head of Tax at Protocol Labs and Vice President of Global Tax at Ripple. Liz is the former Asia-Pacific Head of Tax for Google and was a Tax Director at Softbank Group International. Prior to Google, she was an attorney with the global law firm of Baker & McKenzie LLP in Palo Alto, California, where she advised technology companies on international tax planning and structuring. Liz received her Bachelors and Masters degrees from Stanford University and her Juris Doctor from the University of California, Hastings College of the Law.

332.bio: Huahua (Rene) Zheng is the Co-founder and CEO of CharacterX, and previously was the Head of Product at NXG Labs and Product Manager at Tencent. She holds a Master's degree in East Asian Studies from Stanford University and a Bachelor's degree in English Language and Literature from Fudan University.

334.bio: Simon Morris is the former Chief Strategy Officer at ConsenSys and BitTorrent. He completed his undergraduate degree at the University of Cambridge in the UK and earned his MBA from Stanford University.

335.bio: Derek is the Chief Operating Officer of Bitvenus, having served as Head of operations for multinational companies in Europe and Asia Pacific, and has extensive experience in global operations strategy. He graduated from Stanford University.

337.bio: Ophelia Snyder was previously the co-founder and co-chair of 21Shares. She is a graduate of Stanford University and New York University.

338.bio: Jeremy Fine currently works at OpenAI and was previously Head of BD and CorpDev at OpenSea and Director of Corporate Development at Uber. He graduated in political science from Stanford University.

339.bio: Kristina Campbell is the former CFO of Ripple and holds an MBA from Harvard University.

340.bio: Bernadette Cay was previously the Chief Operating Officer of Linera. She is a graduate of Stanford University.

341.bio: Nickelodeon was previously the chief marketing officer of Circle and has served as a marketing executive at Meta and Google. She graduated from Stanford University and Harvard University.

342.bio: Bobby Jaros was previously the former COO of Oasis. Previously, he was the CEO of Lookflow. Jaros has a Bachelor's degree and Master's degree in Electrical Engineering from Stanford, and an MBA from Stanford Graduate School of Business. He has worked in Machine Learning at Yahoo Labs, Principle Investing at Goldman Sachs, and Strategy at Nokia.

343.bio: Lauren Rothwell is the former co-founder and CEO of Blaze and was previously at Goldman Sachs. She holds an MBA from Stanford University.

344.bio: Tyler Crimm is the former Lead Engineer at Paradigm. He started his career as a software engineer at Salesforce in 2013. Prior to joining, Tyler was an engineer at AppOmni where he focused on client solutions and feature development. Tyler holds a B.S. and M.S. in Computer Science from Stanford University, and an MBA from MIT Sloan.

345.bio: James Loftus is the former Managing Partner at PayPal Ventures.He graduated from Stanford University.

346.bio: Caroline Ellison is the former CEO of Alameda Research.

347.bio: Zack Lawrence was the founder and Chief Technology Officer of Vest and a software engineer at Slock.it. He graduated from Stanford University.

349.bio:
Evan Miyazono is Head of Research at Protocol Labs. He has lived in California his whole life, bouncing between NorCal and SoCal. He spent enough of his childhood at beaches that a sandy shore always feels familiar; the rest was spent sitting at a computer, reading books, or disassembling things. He eventually majored in Materials Science and Engineering at Stanford after unintentionally picking up minors in math and CS. He was then driven by his curiosity to a Ph.D. in Applied Physics at Caltech and, upon completion, was lead to Protocol Labs by a desire to build systems that affect real people.
Evan still loves beaches, reading, and disassembly. Figuring out how things do, should, or could work is a passion, and sharing that knowledge is a favorite hobby. He'll go on for hours if you tell him you're interested in his thesis, so set a time limit before starting on that particular odyssey of quantum optics and nanofabrication.

351.bio: Stephanie Tramicheck was previously the former founder and CEO of Lancelot Labs. She is a cryptocurrency OG with years of experience in blockchain and high-growth tech startups.Stephanie previously served as Chief Marketing Officer at Nifty Gateway (owned by Gemini), and Regional General Manager at Pinterest and Etsy.

352.bio: Jaou Toure previously worked at McKinsey & Company and Huma Finance. She holds an MBA from Stanford University.

353.bio: Yu Rebecca Miao, is the co-founder and CEO of Neomera BioLab, with 15+ years of drug development experience, and was the Chief Safety Officer of AKSO Bio. He is a postdoctoral scholar at Stanford University School of Medicine.

354.bio: Ryan Caldbeck is the former Chief Operating Officer at Dune Analytics. Ryan Caldbeck received their MBA from Stanford University Graduate School of Business between 2003 and 2005. Ryan obtained their BA in Public Policy from Duke University between 1997 and 2001.

355.bio: Yu (Jimmy) Xu is the CTO of Neomera BioLab and a postdoctoral fellow in bioengineering at Stanford University.

358.bio: Gabriel Bianconi is a consultant and former Chief Product Officer at Ondo Finance. Previously, he provided technical consulting, due diligence and proof-of-concept development services for investment firms such as Two Sigma and a large sovereign wealth fund. He is a bachelor's and master's student in Computer Science from Stanford University, where he conducted research in deep learning and computer vision.

1486.bio: Aisha Hunt is the founder of Kelley Hunt, PLLC. She is also a J.D. from Stanford Law School.
